steps.title: Recognize D and E as midpoints
steps.content: Given that D is the midpoint of segment AB and E is the midpoint of segment BC, the segment DE connects the midpoints of two sides of triangle ABC.

steps.title: Apply the Midsegment Theorem
steps.content: When a segment connects the midpoints of two sides of a triangle, it is parallel to the third side and is half the length of that third side. Therefore, DE is parallel to AC.

steps.title: Solve for AC
steps.content: Given that DE = 14, we substitute into the equation: $$14 = \frac{1}{2}AC$$ $$AC = 2 \times 14 = 28$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
